subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 2 players with a single deck.
- **Expansion note** — Additional decks allow 3-4 player or multiplayer formats.
- **Solo mode** — Not included in the base game.

#### Cards
- **Trade Deck** — 80 trade deck cards (ships and bases across 4 factions: Trade Federation, Blob, Star Empire, Machine Cult).
- **Explorer pile** — 10 Explorer cards (always available to purchase).
- **Scout cards** — 20 Scouts (starting cards providing 1 Trade each).
- **Viper cards** — 4 Vipers (starting cards providing 1 Combat each).

#### Tokens
- **Authority cards** — 2 sets of Authority tracking cards (10 and 20 denominations) to track each player's health from 50.

#### Faction Breakdown
- **Trade Federation (blue)** — Focuses on Authority gain and Trade generation.
- **Blob (green)** — Aggressive faction with high Combat output and card draw.
- **Star Empire (yellow)** — Combat plus opponent discard effects.
- **Machine Cult (red)** — Deck thinning through scrapping cards from hand or discard.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Setup
1. **Trade Deck** — Shuffle all 80 trade deck cards into a single face-down deck in the center.
2. **Trade Row** — Reveal 5 cards face up from the Trade Deck to form the Trade Row.
3. **Explorer pile** — Place the 10 Explorer cards face up in a separate stack beside the Trade Row.
4. **Scrap heap** — Designate an area for scrapped cards (removed from the game).
5. **Player areas** — Each player needs space for a draw deck, discard pile, and played cards (in-play area).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Starting deck** — Each player takes 10 cards: 8 Scouts and 2 Vipers.
- **Shuffle** — Each player shuffles their 10-card starting deck.
- **Starting hand** — Each player draws 3 cards for their opening hand (first turn only).
- **Authority** — Each player sets their starting Authority to 50 using the Authority cards.
- **Play area** — Keep a clear space for ships and bases played during your turn.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Turn
- **Determine first player** — Choose randomly.
- **Reduced first hand** — Both players draw only 3 cards for their first hand (not 5).
- **Normal hands after** — From the second turn onward, players draw 5 cards at end of turn.
- **Begin play** — First player takes their turn, then alternate.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Main Phase** — Play cards from hand, activate ally abilities, use base abilities, and acquire cards.
2. **Discard Phase** — Discard all played ships and remaining hand cards to your discard pile.
3. **Draw Phase** — Draw 5 new cards from your personal deck.

#### Main Phase Details
- **Play cards freely** — Play any number of cards from hand in any order.
- **Accumulate resources** — Trade and Combat pool up during your turn.
- **Spend resources** — Use Trade to buy cards, Combat to attack opponent or their bases.
- **Order flexibility** — You may interleave playing, buying, and attacking in any order.
- **Bases persist** — Bases remain in play across turns until destroyed.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Acquiring Cards
- **Buy from Trade Row** — Pay a card's Trade cost to acquire it into your discard pile.
- **Buy Explorers** — Pay 2 Trade to acquire an Explorer at any time.
- **Row refill** — Immediately replace purchased Trade Row cards from the Trade Deck.

#### Dealing Damage
- **Attack opponent** — Spend Combat to reduce opponent's Authority.
- **Attack bases** — Spend Combat equal to a base's defense to destroy it.
- **Outpost priority** — If opponent has an Outpost in play, you must destroy all Outposts before dealing Combat damage to the player or attacking non-Outpost bases.

#### Card Types
- **Ships** — Play for immediate effect, discard at end of turn.
- **Bases** — Play to your base area, remain in play until destroyed by opponent.
- **Outposts** — Bases with a shield icon that must be destroyed before the player can be attacked.

#### Card Abilities
- **Primary ability** — Resolves when the card is played (ships) or first enters play (bases).
- **Ally ability** — Triggers if you have another card of the same faction in play this turn.
- **Scrap ability** — Optional one-time effect gained by permanently removing the card from the game.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Faction Ally Bonuses
- **Trigger condition** — When you play a card, check if another card of the same faction is already in play this turn.
- **Multiple triggers** — Playing a third faction card does not re-trigger the first two; each card's ally ability triggers once when its condition is met.
- **Cross-check** — Bases in play from previous turns count for ally triggers.

#### Scrapping
- **Scrap ability** — Some cards have a scrap icon ability; use it by removing the card from the game entirely.
- **Timing** — Scrap abilities can be used at any point during your Main Phase.
- **Deck thinning** — Scrapping weak starting cards (Scouts, Vipers) improves future draws.

#### Deck Cycling
- **Reshuffle** — When your draw deck is empty, shuffle your discard pile to form a new draw deck.
- **Empty state** — If both deck and discard are empty, you draw no cards.

#### Multi-Faction Cards
- **Dual faction** — Some cards belong to two factions and can trigger ally abilities for either faction.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Authority depletion** — The game ends immediately when a player's Authority reaches 0 or below.

#### Win Condition
- **Last standing** — The player with Authority remaining wins.
- **No VP tally** — There is no point-based scoring; this is a pure elimination game.

#### Timing Note
- **Immediate loss** — Authority can go negative during a turn; the game ends the moment it hits 0.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Faction focus** — Prioritize buying cards from 1-2 factions early to maximize ally ability triggers.
- **Thin your deck** — Use Machine Cult scrap abilities or Explorer scrap to remove Scouts and Vipers.
- **Early bases** — Bases provide recurring value every turn they survive.
- **Trade over Combat early** — Building economy in the first few turns lets you acquire powerful cards before your opponent.
- **Watch the Trade Row** — Deny your opponent key cards that would complete their faction synergies.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Spreading across all 4 factions** — Buying from every faction prevents ally triggers, weakening your deck.
- **Ignoring deck thinning** — A bloated deck full of Scouts draws weak hands late game.
- **Forgetting Outpost rules** — You must destroy all Outposts before attacking the player directly.
- **Hoarding Combat** — Unused Combat at end of turn is wasted; always spend it.
- **Overlooking scrap abilities** — One-time scrap effects on purchased cards can swing pivotal turns.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Aggression vs Economy
- **Rush damage** — Focus on Combat cards to pressure opponent's Authority early.
- **Build engine** — Focus on Trade and card draw to field dominant late-game turns.

#### Decision 2: Faction Commitment
- **Deep faction** — Go heavy into 1 faction for reliable ally triggers.
- **Splash faction** — Add a secondary faction for versatility and to deny opponent.

#### Decision 3: Scrap Timing
- **Scrap now** — Remove a card immediately for its effect when you need the boost.
- **Scrap later** — Keep the card for its primary and ally abilities until the scrap value exceeds ongoing value.
